query: As of today (July 1, 2026), is OpenAI's GPT-5.6 generally available, and is it scored/ranked on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai / DataLearner mirror), or is GPT-5.6 still only a government-gated limited preview announced June 26, 2026? Distinguish clearly between GPT-5.5 (released April 2026, scored on the leaderboard) and GPT-5.6 (limited preview). What is OpenAI's actual highest-scoring generally-available model on the AA Intelligence Index right now, and its score/rank?
answer:
I have completed the research. Here is the summary of findings:

**Bottom line:** As of July 1, 2026, GPT-5.6 is **NOT generally available** and is **NOT scored on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index**. It remains exactly what the question describes — a government-gated limited preview announced June 26, 2026.

**Key distinctions:**

- **GPT-5.6** (Sol/Terra/Luna) — Announced **June 26, 2026** as a limited preview to "a small group of trusted partners" via Codex/API, restricted at the request of the U.S. government. OpenAI promises broad GA "in the coming weeks" but it hasn't happened. It appears on **no** public leaderboard.

- **GPT-5.5** ("Spud") — Released **April 23, 2026**, genuinely GA, and fully scored on the AA Intelligence Index.

**OpenAI's highest-scoring GA model on the AA Intelligence Index:** **GPT-5.5 (xhigh) = 55, ranked #3 overall.** The top of the board is dominated by Anthropic: Claude Fable 5 (60), Claude Opus 4.8 max (56), then GPT-5.5 xhigh (55). This was cross-validated across the Artificial Analysis site directly, the DataLearner mirror (updated 2026-07-01, 216 models), and a BenchLM mirror.

sources.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn't be the norm
sources.snippet: OpenAI is limiting the release of its newest AI models to a 'small group of trusted partners' at the behest of the U.S. government. The GPT-5.6 lineup includes Sol (flagship), Terra (balanced), and Luna (faster, lower-cost).

sources.title: GPT-5.6 Preview System Card - OpenAI Deployment Safety Hub
sources.snippet: GPT-5.6 is a new family of three models: Sol (flagship), Terra (lower-cost), and Luna (fastest). We plan to make GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna generally available in the coming weeks.

sources.title: OpenAI launches a limited preview of GPT-5.6 for a 'small group of trusted partners'
sources.snippet: OpenAI has started previewing its GPT-5.6 series in three versions to a limited number of trusted partners; broader availability in the coming weeks.

sources.title: LLM Leaderboard - Artificial Analysis
sources.snippet: Top of Intelligence Index: Claude Fable 5 (with fallback) 60, Claude Opus 4.8 (max) 56, GPT-5.5 (xhigh) 55, Claude Opus 4.7 (max) 54, Claude Sonnet 5 (max) 53. GPT-5.6 not listed.

sources.title: Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index - AI Model Leaderboard | DataLearnerAI
sources.snippet: Top Model Claude Fable 5, Top Score 60, Model Count 216. Data source: Artificial Analysis. GPT-5.6 not present.

sources.title: GPT-5.5 - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: GPT-5.5 is a large language model released by OpenAI on April 23, 2026, codenamed 'Spud'.

sources.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 just weeks after GPT-5.4 as AI race accelerates
sources.snippet: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 to its paid subscribers on April 23, 2026, six weeks after GPT-5.4.

sources.title: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) - Intelligence, Performance & Price Analysis
sources.snippet: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) is amongst the leading models in intelligence; 922k token context window.

sources.title: Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index Benchmark 2026 | BenchLM.ai
sources.snippet: Claude Opus 4.8 leads at 55.7%, followed by GPT-5.5 (54.8%) and Claude Opus 4.7 (Adaptive) (mirror rounding).

key_facts: GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) was announced June 26, 2026 as a LIMITED PREVIEW to a 'small group of trusted partners' via Codex and API — NOT generally available as of July 1, 2026.
The GPT-5.6 preview was restricted at the request of the U.S. government (Trump administration); OpenAI stated such government access 'should not become the long-term default.'
OpenAI says it plans to make GPT-5.6 generally available 'in the coming weeks' (mid-July 2026 best case per Axios); this had NOT occurred as of July 1, 2026.
GPT-5.6 does NOT appear on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index or its DataLearner mirror (or any other public leaderboard) because it is a gated preview.
GPT-5.5 (codename 'Spud') was released April 23, 2026 to paid subscribers and API — it is generally available and fully scored on the AA Intelligence Index.
OpenAI's highest-scoring generally-available model on the AA Intelligence Index is GPT-5.5 (xhigh) with a score of 55, ranked #3 overall.
AA Intelligence Index top 5 (July 1, 2026): Claude Fable 5 with fallback (60), Claude Opus 4.8 max (56), GPT-5.5 xhigh (55), Claude Opus 4.7 max (54), Claude Sonnet 5 max (53).
GPT-5.5 reasoning variants on AA Index: xhigh=55, high=53, medium=50.
The DataLearner mirror (updated 2026-07-01) lists Claude Fable 5 at 60 as top model across 216 models, sourced from Artificial Analysis, and does not include GPT-5.6.
Anthropic currently dominates the top of the AA Intelligence Index; no OpenAI model outranks GPT-5.5 (xhigh).
